danger_signals.visual_form: TIE fighter silhouettes, Imperial Star Destroyer hulls, red/gray color scheme of Empire vessels
danger_signals.meaning: Active military threat; gameplay objective to neutralize or evade

danger_signals.visual_form: Unique TIE fighter variants, second Death Star structure during missions
Viraxo family freighter and fighter activity in mission briefings and space encounters
danger_signals.meaning: Commercial and personal danger to Azzameen family operations

danger_signals.visual_form: Home station raided with Imperial vessels present, hangar bay visual chaos
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent loss of family assets and lives; prologue story beat

safety_signals.visual_form: X-wing fighter formations, Rebel base structures, blue/white vessel color schemes
safety_signals.meaning: Allied military support; mission objective completion zones

safety_signals.visual_form: Open hangar bay doors, visual docking port, stationary allied vessels
safety_signals.meaning: Resupply and repair safety; tactical regrouping opportunity

safety_signals.visual_form: Azzameen family freighter presence, friendly cargo transports, designated family mission zones
safety_signals.meaning: Commercial safe zone; family business operations area

safety_signals.visual_form: Pre-flight dialogue, mail delivery system, Ace's room with collected souvenirs
safety_signals.meaning: Non-combat narrative safe space; relationship and story development

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Imperial insignia on TIE fighters and capital ships
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Standard TIE fighters, experimental TIE variants, Imperial Star Destroyers, second Death Star
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Imperial base structures, military outposts, blockade formations

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Rebel insignia on X-wings and larger capital vessels
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Rebel base structures, task force formations, safe zones for player hyperjumps

faction_indicators.color_identity: Corellian transport color schemes (likely civilian trading aesthetics)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Family freighter visual presence, home station, family mission briefing locations

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Viraxo freighters and fighters in hostile encounters
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Competitive trading zones, hostile rendezvous points during family missions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increased Imperial patrol activity, desperate Rebel task force deployments, family smuggling operations necessity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ace's father Tomaas forced to smuggle bacta to Alliance outpost; heightened combat frequency

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Home station under raid; destroyed family infrastructure visual cues; presence of Imperial boarding parties
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Family seeks asylum with Rebel task force; family members killed; loss of family mission safety zone

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Massive space station structure appearance in mission space; high-priority target visual designation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mission objectives shift to reconnaissance and assault; increased Rebel task force mobilization

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Climactic battle formation visuals; convergence of Rebel and Imperial fleets; planetary backdrop
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final mission sequence; culmination of story arc; maximum combat intensity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Souvenir collection in Ace's room accumulates; mission difficulty scaling through visual complexity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ace transitions from family trader to significant Rebel pilot; participation in strategic missions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Millennium Falcon cockpit with turret stations visible; multi-part mission region transitions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can delegate AI crew to different stations or direct them; hangar bay visible during in-flight repairs

environmental_storytelling.example: Screenshots feature second Death Star alongside Millennium Falcon cockpit and starship formations, conveying scale of conflict and player's role within larger war narrative
Accumulated items in Ace's room serve as visual record of progression and mission history; each piece represents a story beat or achievement
Family members and Alliance contacts send communications that reveal personal relationships, family feud developments, and strategic situation updates without requiring explicit cutscenes
Floating display windows as part of in-flight heads-up display communicate real-time world-state through radar signatures, damage indicators, and tactical information
Visual presence of different starship classes (fighters, freighters, capital ships) in specific zones communicates faction control, mission objectives, and strategic importance
Ability to witness ongoing battle outside hangar while inside conveys continuous world-state activity and stakes even during tactical regrouping
Multi-part missions with hyperjump transitions between regions create visual sense of expanding operational scope and evolving galactic situation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Formation flight patterns; reactive to player detection; standard aggression levels
Clustered around capital ships or objectives; increased intercept aggression; coordinated evasion
Formation-based cooperative flight; respond to player coordination; defend mission objectives
Non-combat vector flight; vulnerability to interception; prioritized protect objectives
Hostile intercept patterns; contest player cargo or mission objectives; escalate family feud narrative
Stationary or slow-moving; defended by Imperial fleet; objective for reconnaissance and attack missions
AI crew mans uncontrolled positions; responds to player orders for specific stations; engaged combat with turret capability

notes: X-Wing Alliance communicates world-state primarily through vessel visual identity (color, silhouette, formation), mission briefing narrative structure (mail system, family room progression), and spatial encounter design. The game's 1999 context means manifestation relies on model differentiation and HUD information rather than advanced particle or shader effects. Family feud and political conflict are conveyed through mission objectives, NPC dialogue, and story progression rather than dynamic environmental shifts. The transition from trader to military pilot is indicated through mission assignment escalation and vessel access (progression from Corellian transports to X-wings to capital ship multi-crew operations).
